domain: pedology / soil microbiome ecology (mycorrhizal-free decentralized soil microbiome coordination)
specific_mechanism: Soil microbiomes coordinate via dissolved organic compound diffusion through soil pore water; agents (microbes) sense local concentration gradients and respond by altering metabolic output, which changes the gradient others sense; coordination emerges with no broadcast channel and no central coordinator, only local sensing and local emission. The system has 4 functional roles (decomposers, synthesizers, fixators, mineralizers)
llm_application: A decentralized LLM agent pool that coordinates only via 4-role local emissions to a shared embedding-space substrate: each agent senses local embedding density and either emits, consumes, transforms, or fixes a feature; coordination emerges from local-rule interactions, no broadcast. The 4-role partition is structural — each agent's role is fixed at instantiation.
novelty_claim: Existing decentralized LLM multi-agent (AgentNet, etc.) uses message-passing through RAG/DAG graphs. No published 2024-2026 LLM/AI work formalizes 4-role local-emit-and-sense-style coordination in an embedding-space substrate with no message passing.
